query: Iran IAEA cooperation law suspension 2025 2026, current state of Iran nuclear inspections and diplomacy, US Iran nuclear deal negotiations status June July 2026
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on Iran-IAEA cooperation and US-Iran nuclear diplomacy as of July 2026:

**IAEA Cooperation Law (suspended):** Iran's July 2, 2025 law suspending IAEA cooperation remains in force, hardened in 2026 by new parliamentary legislation and a Supreme National Security Council resolution barring inspector access to the bombed sites (Fordow, Natanz, Isfahan). The IAEA halted all safeguards verification after Feb 28, 2026.

**Current inspection state:** Inspectors have access only to Bushehr and the Tehran research reactor — not the attacked enrichment facilities. The IAEA has lost continuity of knowledge on Iran's ~9,875 kg stockpile (including 440.9 kg of 60% HEU last verified June 13, 2025). Grossi says inspections "will take place"; Iran says only after a final deal.

**Diplomacy status:** Following a major US/Israel-Iran war (Feb–June 2026, during which Khamenei was killed), the two sides signed the **Islamabad Memorandum on June 17, 2026**, opening a 60-day window (to ~mid-August) for a final deal. Post-war nuclear talks were held in Lucerne (June 21–22) and Doha (June 30–July 2), with the next round expected the third week of July. Core disputes remain: inspection access/timing, whether down-blended uranium is shipped abroad, Strait of Hormuz, and the $6bn frozen-funds release. The US Treasury waived oil/petrochemical sanctions through Aug 21, 2026.

The full report, sources, and structured data points have been recorded via StructuredOutput.

key_facts: Iran's law suspending IAEA cooperation was signed by President Pezeshkian on July 2, 2025; passed unanimously by parliament and endorsed by the Guardian Council. It halts inspections 'until the security of the nuclear facilities is guaranteed.'
In 2026, Iran hardened its stance with new parliamentary legislation and a Supreme National Security Council resolution barring IAEA inspector access to bombed sites (Fordow, Natanz, Isfahan).
Per the IAEA's June 4, 2026 report, the agency stopped all safeguards verification activities in Iran after February 28, 2026; continuity of knowledge on the stockpile has been lost.
As of early July 2026, IAEA inspectors have access only to the Bushehr power plant and Tehran research reactor — not the attacked enrichment facilities.
A major US/Israel-Iran war ran Feb 28–mid-June 2026; Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was killed Feb 28, 2026, and negotiator Ali Larijani was also assassinated.

The Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding was signed remotely by Trump and Pezeshkian on June 17, 2026, setting a 60-day window (to ~mid-August) for a final nuclear deal; Iran pledged never to acquire nuclear weapons and the US pledged to remove all sanctions upon a final agreement.
First post-war nuclear negotiations round was held in Lake Lucerne, Switzerland on June 21-22, 2026; a second round was held in Doha June 30-July 2, 2026.

The July 2, 2026 Doha talks produced agreement on a communication channel and spending procedures for $6 billion in frozen Iranian funds, but no breakthrough on core issues. Next round expected third week of July 2026 after Khamenei's funeral.

US and Iran give conflicting accounts of the inspection issue: Grossi says IAEA inspections 'will indeed take place' and the MoU provides for them 'explicitly'; Iran says there is 'no plan for access to attacked facilities' and inspections only after a final deal.

Last IAEA-verified stockpile (June 13, 2025): ~9,875 kg total enriched uranium and 440.9 kg of 60% enriched (near weapons-grade) uranium. Most 60% HEU believed sealed in the Isfahan/Esfahan tunnel complex.
The MoU commits both sides to down-blend stockpiled enriched material under IAEA oversight, but they dispute whether it must be shipped abroad (US) or retained in Iran (Iran's position).
US Treasury issued licenses allowing Iranian petrochemical and oil sales through August 21, 2026 (60-day waiver); full sanctions removal is contingent on a final nuclear agreement.
Negotiation format is disputed: US calls Doha meetings 'direct talks'; Iran insists they are 'technical talks' mediated by Qatar and Pakistan.

IAEA safeguards verification activities halted in Iran since
